# llm-client

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 44
- **Lines of code**: 8,061
- **Languages**: TypeScript (42), JSON (2)
- **Zones**: 3 (3 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 3acc839
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-10T02:45:58.083Z

## Architecture Zones

### LLM Client Core (`llm-client-core`)

The complete vendor-neutral LLM client library: provider interface, registry, session management, Claude and Codex adapters, auth, config, token usage, process execution, and shared CLI utilities.

- Files: 36
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Entry points: src/config.ts, src/json.ts, src/project-config.ts, src/suggest.ts, src/types.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/llm-client-core/context.md`

### LLM Client Tests (`llm-client-tests`)

Unit tests covering the five public-API entry points: config, JSON serialization, project-config overrides, CLI typo suggestion, and shared types.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/llm-client-tests/context.md`

### Package Config (`package-config`)

Top-level package manifest and build tooling configuration for the llm-client package.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/package-config/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/types.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/config.ts` (6 imports)
- `src/create-client.ts` (4 imports)
- `src/provider-registry.ts` (4 imports)
- `src/token-usage.ts` (4 imports)
- `src/api-provider.ts` (3 imports)
- `src/cli-provider.ts` (3 imports)
- `src/codex-cli-provider.ts` (3 imports)
- `src/exec.ts` (3 imports)
- `src/auth.ts` (2 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 20 files)
- `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (used by 3 files)

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- CompletionRequest (types.ts) contains CLI-provider-specific fields cliFlags and timeoutMs. This shared contract type, imported by all domain packages, is contaminated with implementation details from one provider backend. CLI-specific options should move to CliProviderOptions and be excluded from the vendor-neutral request type. [warning] (llm-client-core)
- The codex entry in createDefaultRegistry() constructs an inline LLMProvider adapter around createCodexCliClient rather than the codex provider implementing LLMProvider directly. Registry code is supposed to be a pure dispatch table with no provider logic; the shim belongs in codex-cli-provider.ts. [warning] (llm-client-core)
- Two independent vendor selection paths exist: createLLMClient() in llm-client.ts and ProviderRegistry.getActiveProvider() in provider-registry.ts. They implement the same vendor-resolution logic (config.vendor ?? 'claude') without delegating to each other, so a new vendor must be registered in both places to be fully supported. [warning] (llm-client-core)
- createLLMClient() returns ClaudeClient instead of LLMProvider. Callers of the 'vendor-neutral' factory receive a Claude-typed value, which prevents swapping vendors transparently and exposes Claude-specific interface members (e.g. mode: AuthMode) to vendor-agnostic consumers. Should return LLMProvider to honour the abstraction boundary. [warning] (llm-client-core)
- spawnTool() and spawnManaged() each contain a near-verbatim copy of the SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation timer block (~15 lines). Only the constant KILL_ESCALATION_MS is shared. The duplicated logic should be extracted into a private helper to prevent the two paths from drifting apart on future changes. [info] (llm-client-core)

### Patterns

- 86% of llm-client-core files (31/36) have no corresponding cross-zone import from the test zone, concentrating unit-test risk on the provider stack and execution layer — the most behaviorally complex parts of the package. [warning] (llm-client-core)

### Relationships

- llm-client-core acts as the single hub for all intra-package cross-zone flows: it is consumed by llm-client-tests and shaped by package-config, with no reciprocal imports — a clean spoke-to-hub dependency direction. [info] (llm-client-core)
- llm-client-tests imports exclusively from utility/config modules in llm-client-core, creating a structural split: utility logic is unit-tested via cross-zone imports while provider and execution logic is not — any regression in those paths requires integration-level detection. [warning] (llm-client-tests)
- package-config governs llm-client-core and llm-client-tests via build tooling (tsconfig paths, vitest globals) with zero import edges — this out-of-band coupling is structurally sound but invisible to import-graph analysis tools. [info] (package-config)

### Observations

- Size imbalance: largest zone has 36 files vs smallest with 3 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Contains 82% of project files (36/44) — may be too broad, consider splitting [warning] (llm-client-core)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (llm-client-core)
- General-purpose utilities (exec.ts, help-format.ts, suggest.ts, output.ts) are co-located with LLM-specific infrastructure; if these utilities are consumed by orchestration scripts rather than LLM consumers, extracting them into a separate utility zone would improve single-responsibility clarity. [info] (llm-client-core)
- Perfect cohesion score (1.0) confirms all 36 files are fully interconnected within the zone — appropriate for a shared foundation package with a single public API surface. [info] (llm-client-core)
- Zero coupling score reflects the architectural invariant that llm-client imports no sibling packages — it is the DAG root, as documented in public.ts. [info] (llm-client-core)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (llm-client-tests)
- 1:1 mapping between test files and declared src entry points indicates the test suite is scoped to the package's public API boundary rather than implementation internals — a sustainable, refactor-friendly pattern. [info] (llm-client-tests)
- No test files exist for the provider stack (api-provider, cli-provider, codex-cli-provider) or the exec/auth modules within this zone; confirm these are covered by integration tests to avoid coverage gaps in the most complex parts of the package. [info] (llm-client-tests)
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling in both zones means the test boundary is clean — tests import only from src, and src has no test-layer dependencies. [info] (llm-client-tests)
- ... and 4 more

### Suggestions

- Establish an explicit naming rule for test files that distinguishes unit tests covering public API surface ('types.test.ts', 'config.test.ts') from those covering implementation details — currently the test zone covers only 5 of 22 src files with no documented rationale for the selection, making it unclear whether the remaining 17 are intentionally untested at unit level or simply missed. [info]
- Consolidate 'llm-types.ts' and 'types.ts' into a single file or establish an explicit naming rule distinguishing them (e.g. 'types.ts' for vendor-neutral shared types, 'llm-types.ts' for provider-facing contracts); coexistence without a documented split invites accidental duplication of type definitions. [warning] (llm-client-core)
- Rename 'api-provider.ts' → 'claude-api-provider.ts' and 'cli-provider.ts' → 'claude-cli-provider.ts' to match the 'codex-cli-provider.ts' naming pattern; all three are vendor-specific and the asymmetric naming implies api-provider/cli-provider are vendor-neutral when they are not. [warning] (llm-client-core)
- Rename 'provider-interface.ts' to 'llm-provider.ts' — the filename suffix '-interface' describes the TypeScript construct rather than the domain concept, inconsistent with every other file in the zone which names the domain entity. [info] (llm-client-core)
- Rename 'suggest.ts' to a name that communicates its domain role (e.g. 'config-suggest.ts' or 'value-suggest.ts') — 'suggest' is too generic and does not self-document within an LLM client package. [info] (llm-client-core)
- Add a unit test file for 'types.ts' (CompletionRequest, CompletionResponse, LLMProvider interface shape) — this is the cross-package contract type consumed by every downstream package, and it currently has no unit-level regression guard in the test zone. [warning] (llm-client-tests)
- Rename zone to 'llm-client-config' to match the 'llm-client-*' prefix convention used by the other two zones; inconsistent zone naming makes tooling output harder to scan at a glance. [info] (package-config)
- Zone "Package Config" (package-config) has at-risk risk (score: 0.50, cohesion: 0.00, coupling: 0.00) — unreliable: zone has only 3 files (minimum 5 for reliable metrics) [info] (package-config)

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
